other_observers: Steve Messick; Dave Leatherman found the bird on June 2 in the same location. Nick Komar, Nick A. Komar, Rachel Hopper, Glenn Walbeck, and Eric Defonso are some other observers of this bird.
specific_location: Crow Valley Campground, about 100 yards west of the picnic tables.
description: Light brownish orange head, neck, back, and tail. Large white eye ring. Cheek darker than the head and body. Dark eye. Bright white belly and breast with large dark spots. Short tail. Pink legs.Behaviors: It usually stayed low in the bushes. When I got to see it the first time it was thrashing around on the ground under a tree. Then it flew out of the bushes into a tree where it didnt move for a few minutes. Then it flew into a different tree and sat there for about ten minutes. Then it flew and gave the call into another tree and I never saw or heard it after that.Call: It gave a pit pit pit call when it was in flight and a few times after it landed in a tree. It also gave a series of gurgling phrases. It was very distinctive and something I had never heard before this bird. I knew it was nothing I was familiar with.Plumage: Adult
